override.title: Made by hand, made to last
Gear that keeps up with you

name: Deal of the Day — Artisan Classic

override.title: Deal of the day
override.description: One handmade piece, one honest price — today only. Each item is shaped, finished and checked in small batches, so when the timer runs out, this offer is gone for good.

override.title: 48-hour flash deal
override.description: Our steepest markdown of the month on a customer favourite. Stock is limited to what is in the warehouse right now — once the countdown hits zero, the price goes back up.

override.title: The limited-edition drop
override.description: A signature piece released in a strictly limited run, offered at a private-sale price for one week only. When these are spoken for, we will not be recasting them.

override.title: Floor-model clearance
override.description: Showroom pieces in excellent condition are moving out to make room for the new collection. Save on designs we will never restock — delivery is on us while the countdown runs.

override.title: Today's headline deal
override.description: The piece everyone asked about, at its lowest price this season. Pair it with the doorbusters below — everything ships together, and the timer applies to all of it.

override.title: The weekend mega deal
override.description: Every weekend we put one hero product on the block. This week it is a bestseller with a five-star track record — same warranty, same support, a fraction of the usual price.

override.title: Backed by the brands you trust
The seasonal drop ends soon
override.description: This season's capsule is on the rail for one more week. Sizes are already thinning out in the most-loved styles — when the countdown ends, the capsule is archived for good.

override.title: Every department, marked down
override.description: Storewide savings with a hard deadline. Pick your department below and stack the sale price with free returns — no code needed, the discount is already on the tag.

override.title: Pick a rail, find a bargain
This week's featured deal
override.description: Seven days, one standout product, one serious price cut. We restock it once — if the counter reaches zero or the shelf empties first, the deal retires until next season.

name: The Hands Behind Every Piece — Image Left

override.title:
The hands behind
every piece
override.body: Every object in our shop starts on a workbench, not a production line. Small batches, natural materials, and techniques passed down through generations mean no two pieces are ever exactly alike — and that is the point.

override.title:
Cut, set and finished
entirely by hand
override.body: Each stone is inspected under a loupe before it ever meets a setting. Our goldsmiths shape, solder and polish every band in-house, so the piece you unbox has already passed through a dozen careful hands.

override.title:
Furniture that outlives
trends — and decades
override.body: We source certified timber, finish with low-VOC oils, and design joints that can be repaired instead of replaced. A piece that stays in your home for thirty years is the most sustainable piece we can make.

override.title:
Real support,
from real humans
override.body: Setup help, firmware questions, warranty claims — our in-house tech team answers within one business day, no scripts and no runaround. Every device ships with a straightforward 24-month warranty.

override.title:
Clean formulas,
proven rituals
override.body: Dermatologist-tested, fragrance-transparent and never tested on animals. We publish the full ingredient list for every product — including the percentages that other labels leave off the jar.

name: Meet the Makers — Handicraft Image Right

override.title:
Meet the makers
behind the bench
override.body: Twelve artisans, one shared workshop, and a rule we never break: nothing leaves the bench until the person who made it would keep it. Follow along on the blog for works in progress and studio notes.

name: Store FAQ — Shipping and Returns Rich Text

override.html:
Questions, answered

How long does delivery take?

Orders placed before 2pm ship the same day. Standard delivery arrives in 2–4 business days; express arrives next day in most regions.

What is your return policy?

You have 30 days from delivery to return any unused item in its original packaging for a full refund. Returns are free — a prepaid label is included with every order.

Do you ship internationally?

Yes. International orders ship tracked and typically arrive within 7–12 business days. Duties are calculated at checkout so there are no surprises at the door.

Still curious? Browse the full FAQ or get in touch — we reply within one business day.

name: Ring Size Guide — Jewellery Rich Text

override.html:
Find your ring size

Wrap a strip of paper snugly around the base of your finger, mark where it overlaps, then measure the length in millimetres. Match it below:

  • Size 5 — 49.3 mm circumference (15.7 mm diameter)
  • Size 6 — 51.9 mm circumference (16.5 mm diameter)
  • Size 7 — 54.4 mm circumference (17.3 mm diameter)
  • Size 8 — 57.0 mm circumference (18.1 mm diameter)
  • Size 9 — 59.5 mm circumference (18.9 mm diameter)

Measure at the end of the day when fingers are warm, and size up if you sit between two measurements or the band is wider than 6 mm.

Between sizes or buying a surprise gift? Ask us — every ring includes one free resize within 60 days.

name: Garment Care Instructions — Fashion Rich Text

override.html:
Make it last: garment care

Great clothes age well when they are cared for. A few habits that keep our fabrics looking new for years:

  • Wash cold, wash less. Machine wash at 30°C inside-out. Denim and knitwear rarely need washing after every wear — air them instead.
  • Skip the dryer. Lay knits flat and hang wovens to dry; heat is what shrinks and fades.
  • Store folded. Heavy knits stretch on hangers — fold them on a shelf.
  • Treat stains early. Blot with cold water first; hot water sets most stains.

Fabric-specific instructions are printed on every care label, and our team is happy to advise — send us a note.

override.title:
From a garage kiln
to your living room
override.body: What began as weekend pottery in a borrowed garage is now a full workshop of ceramicists, weavers and woodworkers. The scale changed. The way we make things did not.

override.html:
What we believe

Handmade is not a style — it is a standard. It means a person, not a machine, decided when a piece was finished. It means slight variations in glaze and grain are signatures, not defects. And it means that when you buy from us, most of what you pay goes to the person who made the piece.

We fire in small batches, restock slowly, and would rather sell out than rush. Thank you for shopping at the speed of craft.

name: Fit and Size Guide Spread — Fashion

override.title:
Find your size
in sixty seconds
override.body: Our pieces are cut true to size with a relaxed shoulder and a tailored waist. Grab a soft tape measure, check the chart, and you will land the right fit on the first order.

override.html:
How to measure

  • Bust: measure around the fullest point, tape parallel to the floor.
  • Waist: measure at the natural waistline — the narrowest part of your torso.
  • Hips: stand with feet together and measure around the fullest point.
  • Inseam: measure from the top of your inner thigh to your ankle bone.

Between two sizes? Size up for outerwear and knits, down for structured dresses. Exchanges are always free, so ordering two sizes to try at home is completely fine — see how exchanges work.

override.title:
Solid wood, wool
and honest finishes
override.body: Oak and ash from managed forests, wool upholstery that wears in rather than out, and hand-applied oil finishes you can renew yourself. We choose materials that improve with a decade of daily use.

override.html:
Caring for solid wood

Oiled timber asks for very little: dust with a dry cloth, wipe spills promptly, and keep pieces out of direct afternoon sun. Once or twice a year, refresh the surface with a thin coat of furniture oil applied along the grain — ten minutes of work that resets the finish to day one.

Small dents and scratches are part of a piece's life, but most can be lifted with a damp cloth and a warm iron. For anything bigger, write to us — we keep matching oil and parts for every piece we have ever sold.

name: Boutique Hero with Designer Logo Strip

intro.title: A boutique for people who dress with intention.
intro.body: We buy in small runs from designers we actually know, so nothing on this rail is everywhere. Every season we keep the pieces that earn their place in a working wardrobe and let the rest go — which is why our sale rack is small and our repeat customers are not.

intro.title: Every piece in our shop passed through a maker's hands first.
intro.body: We work directly with independent workshops — potters, weavers, carvers — and pay them before a single item ships. That means honest prices, short production runs, and homeware with a story you can actually trace. When a batch is gone, it is gone.

intro.title: Furniture should outlast the trend that sold it.
intro.body: We stock designs chosen for proportion, joinery, and materials that age well — solid oak, full-grain leather, tightly woven linen. Each piece is tested in real homes before it earns a place in the showroom, and every frame carries a ten-year guarantee.

intro.title: Jewellery marks the moment. Ours is made to keep it.
intro.body: Every stone is hand-set in our own atelier, every band finished twice, and every design produced in numbered runs. From first anniversaries to hard-won promotions, we make pieces meant to be worn daily and handed down eventually — with free resizing for life.

sale.title: The solitaire edit — this week only

intro.title: Tech moves fast. Our prices move faster.
intro.body: We track street prices daily and negotiate directly with distributors, so the number on the tag is the lowest we can make it — no coupon hunting required. Every product ships with local warranty, thirty-day returns, and setup support from people who actually use the gear.

intro.title: Groceries the way your week actually works.
intro.body: Order by noon and it is on your counter by dinner. We restock produce every morning, price staples against the big chains weekly, and never substitute without asking. One basket, one delivery window, zero wandering the aisles after work.

intro.title: Forty-eight hours. One wardrobe reset.
intro.body: Every weekend we clear the racks to make room for the next drop — full outfits, not leftovers. Sizes run out in order of popularity, so the earlier you shop, the better the pick. Doors open Friday at nine and everything returns to full price Monday morning.

intro.title: Furniture built for real rooms and long leases.
intro.body: Flat-packed where it makes sense, pre-assembled where it matters, and delivered to the room you point at. Our range is designed around apartment dimensions — narrow stairwells included — with modular pieces that move house as easily as you do.

name: Artisan Story Banner + Maker Logo Strip

intro.title: We built this shop to keep small workshops working.
intro.body: Mass production made things cheaper; it also made them forgettable. We partner with family-run studios that still cut, throw, and stitch by hand, then handle the photography, shipping, and returns so they can keep making. Buy once, keep it for years, know exactly who made it.

intro.title: Everything you need between your front door and the finish line.
intro.body: We are riders first, retailers second. Every bike we sell is assembled and safety-checked in-house, every helmet meets current certification, and our fit advice comes from people who logged their kilometres this morning. First service is on us, always.

override.html:
Quietly obsessed with good rooms

Our buyers spend the year visiting small workshops and family-run mills so that every lamp, chair and textile on our shelves has a story worth telling. We stock fewer pieces than most stores — on purpose — and stand behind each one with a five-year structural guarantee.

Curious how we choose what makes the cut? Read about our sourcing standards.

items.quote: We renovated a 1930s terrace and needed furniture that would not fight the bones of the house. The team here talked me out of two purchases and into better ones — who does that? Eight months later the reading chair by the bay window is still the most argued-over seat in the family, the walnut sideboard has survived two toddlers, and the linen curtains have faded exactly as beautifully as promised. This is what buying for the long term is supposed to feel like.
items.author: Margaret Ellison

items.quote: I style interiors for a living, which makes me a nightmare customer — I notice every seam, every finish, every millimetre of veneer. Nothing I have ordered from this shop has needed to go back. The pieces photograph honestly, the wood is the wood in the pictures, and lead times are quoted straight. I now send clients here without a second thought, and I do not lend my name lightly.

name: Atelier Assurance — Why Shop With Us

override.html:
Jewellery you can trust before you try it on

Every stone we set is independently certified, every metal hallmarked, and every finished piece photographed on the actual item you receive — not a studio stand-in. If it sparkles on your screen, it sparkles in your hands.

Your purchase arrives insured, in a signature gift box, with a lifetime free-polishing promise. Resizing within 30 days is on us.

Browse the collection or book a private consultation.
